2605.24162.title: Knowledge Graph Modulated Deep Learning for Limited-Sample Clinical Data Analysis
2605.24162.paper_summary: Biological systems are governed by structured molecular interactions, where pathways, regulatory circuits, and functional gene relationships shape cellular behavior and disease progression. Much of this knowledge is naturally represented as graphs. However, most biomedical AI models cannot directly use graph-encoded biological knowledge and instead require compressed low-dimensional representations, which can lose important structure and reduce performance, especially in limited-sample clinical studies. Here, we introduce Graph-in-Graph (GiG), a knowledge graph-modulated deep learning framework for data-efficient clinical prediction. GiG represents each patient as a standalone modular graph, in which curated biological knowledge graphs define edges and patient-specific measurements, such as gene expression, define node features. This design allows multiple biological knowledge graphs to be integrated while preserving gene-gene interactions and pathway topology during patient-level representation learning. Across cohorts comprising nearly 9,700 patients and five clinical tasks, including liquid biopsy cancer detection, prostate cancer diagnosis, and 32-class pan-cancer classification, GiG consistently outperforms traditional and state-of-the-art methods, with the largest gains in limited-sample settings. On the challenging prostate cancer diagnosis task, GiG improves macro-F1 by up to 49 percentage points relative to competing methods. Control experiments replacing real pathway graphs with random topologies confirm that these gains arise from biologically grounded knowledge graph structure rather than graph modeling alone. These findings show that knowledge graph-modulated deep learning can improve robustness, interpretability, and sample efficiency in clinical data analysis, and provide a principled framework for integrating biological knowledge graphs into predictive modeling.
2605.24162.paper_summary_zh: 生物系統受到結構化分子互動的支配，其中途徑、調控電路和功能基因關係塑造了細胞行為和疾病進展。這些知識大多以圖形的形式自然呈現。然而，大多數生物醫學人工智慧模型無法直接使用圖形編碼的生物知識，而是需要壓縮的低維表示，這可能會丟失重要結構並降低性能，特別是在有限樣本的臨床研究中。在此，我們介紹了Graph-in-Graph (GiG)，這是一種知識圖譜調制的深度學習框架，用於數據高效的臨床預測。GiG將每位患者表示為一個獨立的模塊圖，其中策劃的生物知識圖定義邊緣，而患者特定的測量（如基因表達）則定義節點特徵。這種設計允許多個生物知識圖的整合，同時在患者級表示學習過程中保留基因-基因互動和途徑拓撲。在包括近9,700名患者和五個臨床任務的隊列中，包括液體活檢癌症檢測、前列腺癌診斷和32類泛癌症分類，GiG始終優於傳統和最先進的方法，在有限樣本環境中獲得最大的增益。在具有挑戰性的前列腺癌診斷任務中，GiG相對於競爭方法將宏觀F1提高了多達49個百分點。控制實驗將真實的途徑圖替換為隨機拓撲，確認這些增益來自於生物學基礎的知識圖結構，而非僅僅是圖建模。這些發現顯示，知識圖譜調制的深度學習可以改善臨床數據分析的穩健性、可解釋性和樣本效率，並提供一個原則性的框架以將生物知識圖整合到預測建模中。
2605.24162.author: Yuwei Xue et.al.
2605.24162.authors: Yuwei Xue, Sakib Mostafa, James Zou, Joseph Liao, Maximilian Diehn, Ash A. Alizadeh, Lei Xing, Md. Tauhidul Islam
